instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding onto a sturdy object or suspension trainer with your arms extended.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your knees behind your toes.
Step:3 From the squat position, pull your body up towards the object or suspension trainer, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 Once you reach the bottom of the squat, explode upwards by jumping off the ground.
Step:4 As you jump, extend your hips, knees, and ankles, pushing through your toes.
Step:5 Land softly back into the squat position and immediately repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a large step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your knees and hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg forward for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the cable handle with an underhand grip, palm facing down.
Step:3 Keep your elbow close to your side and slowly curl your forearm up towards your shoulder.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Step:3 Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your left leg straight.
Step:4 Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side, stepping with your left foot.

DB Box Step-Up.apiName: dumbbell step up single leg balance with bicep curl
instructions: Step:1 Stand in front of a step or platform with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Step:2 Place your right foot on the step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Step:3 Engage your core and push through your right heel to lift your body up onto the step, bringing your left knee up towards your chest.
Step:4 At the top of the movement, perform a bicep curl by bending your elbows and bringing the dumbbells towards your shoulders.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbells back down and simultaneously lower your left foot back to the ground.
Step:6 Repeat the movement on the opposite side, stepping up with your left foot and curling the dumbbells.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells towards the ground, allowing your knees to bend slightly.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then push through your heels and engage your glutes to return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

DB Single-Leg Deadlift.apiName: dumbbell single leg deadlift with stepbox support
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand.
Step:2 Place your left foot on a stepbox or elevated surface behind you.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight and core engaged, hinge forward at the hips, lowering the dumbbell towards the ground.
Step:4 As you lower the dumbbell, simultaneously lift your left leg behind you, maintaining a straight line from head to heel.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbell until you feel a stretch in your right hamstring, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

Single-Leg Glute Bridge.apiName: single leg bridge with outstretched leg
instructions: Step:1 Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Extend one leg straight out in front of you.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Hold for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Lift your left foot off the ground and extend it forward, keeping it parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Slowly lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your right foot flat on the ground and your left leg extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Step:3 Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 As you push back up, lift one leg off the ground and kick it out to the side, keeping it straight.
Step:4 Lower your leg back down and repeat the push-up, then switch to the other leg.
Step:5 Continue alternating leg kicks with each push-up repetition.
Step:1 Place your hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, with your feet elevated on a stable surface.
Step:2 Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe, engaging your core muscles.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your core engaged.
Step:3 Push back up to the starting position.
Step:4 Shift your weight onto your left hand and rotate your body to the right, lifting your right arm towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Hold the side plank position for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat the push-up and side plank on the opposite side.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and dip your body down, then explosively extend your legs and press the dumbbells overhead.
Step:3 Lock out your arms at the top of the movement, then lower the dumbbells back to shoulder level.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 As you push back up, lift one leg off the ground and kick it out to the side, keeping it straight.
Step:4 Lower your leg back down and repeat the push-up, then switch to the other leg.
Step:5 Continue alternating leg kicks with each push-up repetition.
Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Set up a bar at waist height or use a suspension trainer.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grab the bar or handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Perform with extended intensity.
Step:1 Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your back straight as you walk forward, maintaining the dumbbell overhead.
Step:4 Continue walking for the desired distance or time.
Step:5 Switch hands and repeat the exercise.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:3 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

DB Bicep Curl.apiName: dumbbell bicep curl lunge with bowling motion
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, bending your right knee and lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 As you lunge forward, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 At the bottom of the lunge, rotate your torso to the right, as if you were bowling a ball.
Step:5 Reverse the motion, stepping back with your right foot and returning to the starting position while lowering the dumbbells back down.
Step:6 Repeat the lunge and curl motion, this time rotating your torso to the left.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

DB External Rotation.apiName: prone dumbbell upright shoulder external rotation
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, keeping your elbows slightly bent. Emphasize prone control.
Step:3 Rotate your arms externally, bringing the dumbbells up towards your head while keeping your elbows in the same position.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Lie face down on a mat with your legs extended and your toes resting on top of the stability ball.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground, shoulder-width apart, and engage your core muscles.
Step:3 Keeping your legs straight, slowly lift them off the ground, using your lower back and glutes to raise them as high as possible.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start by lying face down on a stability ball with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes touching the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Step:3 Engage your core muscles and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Rotate your torso to one side, bringing your shoulder towards your hip. Keep your hips and legs stable throughout the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat the rotation to the other side.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:3 As you lower your body, simultaneously lift your right arm and left leg off the ground, extending them straight out.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then lower your arm and leg back down while pushing yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat the movement, this time lifting your left arm and right leg.
Step:6 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Scapular Wall Slide.apiName: wall style dumbbell pullover hip extension on exercise ball
instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and the dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the exercise ball down your back until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight and maintaining control.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 While keeping your arms extended, lift your hips off the ground, squeezing your glutes and engaging your core.
Step:7 (wall variation) Lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Step:2 Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, with your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
Step:4 Hold this position for the desired amount of time.
Step:5 Lower your hips back down to the ground and repeat on the other side.
Step:1 Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Step:2 Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, with your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
Step:4 Hold this position for the desired amount of time.
Step:5 Lower your hips back down to the ground and repeat on the other side.
Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down.
Step:2 Rest the dumbbells on your thighs and lean back onto the bench.
Step:3 Use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, then rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Step:4 Exhale and slowly raise the dumbbells above your head, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your arms extended towards the ceiling.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lift your legs off the ground, creating a 90-degree angle at your hips and knees.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower back to press your lower back into the ground.
Step:4 Slowly lower your right arm and left leg towards the ground, keeping them straight and hovering just above the floor.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat the movement with your left arm and right leg.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Start in a plank position with your forearms on the roller and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Engage your core and slowly roll the roller forward, extending your body as far as you can while maintaining control.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the furthest point, then slowly roll the roller back towards your starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at waist height.
Step:2 Stand perpendicular to the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the band handle with both hands and step away from the anchor point to create tension in the band.
Step:4 Bring your hands to your chest, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Step:5 Engage your core and maintain a stable stance.
Step:6 Extend your arms straight out in front of you, pushing the band away from your body.
Step:7 Hold the extended position for a few seconds, focusing on maintaining tension in your core.
Step:8 Slowly bring your hands back to your chest, resisting the pull of the band.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands on your hips.
Step:2 Shift your weight to one leg and lift the opposite leg out to the side, keeping it straight.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat on the other side.
Step:5 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
